business.headline: Iran war hit your portfolio? Track these 4 signals to stay ahead on Dalal StreetFor investors, the bigger challenge is not just falling markets, but the confusion around what to track. Headlines are changing rapidly, with mixed signals on escalation, oil prices and supply disruptions. This makes it difficult to take clear investment decisions.

Getting 8.2% on small savings? Don't invest before these 5 key checksGetting returns of up to 8.2% on small savings schemes may sound like an easy win. But before you lock your money in, it's worth pausing to check a few basics that could impact your finances later.

Income tax filing 2026: Which 5 key links should every taxpayer check now?Filing your income tax return in 2026 may feel a bit different this time. With the portal revamped and new rules in place, knowing where to click can save both time and effort. Here are five key links you should not miss.

Layoffs everywhere, but this company says no job cutsThe company has said it will not cut jobs despite ongoing global uncertainty. It has committed to retaining its workforce and continuing salary payments without delay.
Good Friday 2026: Is stock market open or closed today?Both stock exchanges, the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE) and the National Stock Exchange (NSE), are closed on April 3 on account of Good Friday.

Why buying diamonds is expensive but fails as an investmentThe biggest issue with diamonds is the resale value. Unlike gold, which can be sold easily at market-linked prices, diamonds do not have a strong resale market.

Govt puts mandatory 60% free airline seat rule on hold amid jet fuel price riseThe government has put on hold its directive requiring airlines to offer at least 60% of seats without additional charges, pending further review.

Economic costs are increasing every day: Dr Sajjid Chinoy on impact of Iran warDr Sajjid Z Chinoy, MD and Chief India Economist at JP Morgan, discussed the escalating economic impact of the war on global and Indian markets.

Good Friday holiday: Will NSE and BSE be open or closed on April 3?The stock market holiday list for 2026 includes a total of 16 non-trading days, excluding weekends. With several holidays already behind us, there are still multiple breaks lined up over the coming months.

Gold slips over RS 5,000, silver plunges Rs 15,000: What's behind the sudden fall?Precious metals, often seen as safe bets in uncertain times, have unexpectedly slipped. The latest drop in gold and silver prices has left investors wondering what's driving the decline despite global tensions.

Form 15G, 15H gone: Will new Form 121 simplify TDS for taxpayers?Form 121 is a self-declaration that allows individuals to request that no TDS be deducted on certain types of income. This usually includes interest earned from bank deposits, post office schemes, or similar sources.

Will the new Income Tax website make filing returns simpler for you?The new website brings together all income tax-related information in one place. According to the department, it is easier to navigate, faster to use, and built to reduce confusion for taxpayers who often struggle with forms, rules and procedures.

8th Pay Commission moves ahead: Will Dehradun meeting bring your salary hike sooner?There's finally some activity around the 8th Pay Commission, and it's giving employees a reason to pay attention. With the Dehradun meeting scheduled, many are wondering, will this bring your salary revision any closer?

Gold, silver prices today: Why is silver falling more than gold?On the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), gold futures were trading at Rs 1,49,283 at 11:15 am, down Rs 4,425 or 2.88%. Silver futures saw a steeper fall. Prices were at Rs 2,28,891, down Rs 14,610 or 6.00%.

India's retail boom is hiding a Rs 2,000 crore problemIndia's retail boom is moving faster than ever on the surface, but inside, slow and messy inventory movement is quietly holding back efficiency, profitability and the next phase of growth.

Government waives customs duty on key petrochemical imports amid West Asia warThe Finance Ministry has reduced duty to nil on 40 petrochemical products, with the exemption coming into effect from April 2 and remaining in force till June 30, 2026.

Sensex crashes after Trump's speech on Iran war; Rs 10 lakh crore wiped outThe stock market saw a sharp sell-off, with the Sensex falling over 1,500 points and the Nifty dropping over2%, slipping below the 22,200 mark.

However, the film's familiar plot and cautious storytelling make it an entertaining yet ultimately forgettable sequel.

Hailee Steinfeld, partner Josh Allen welcome first child, a baby girl Hailee Steinfeld and her husband Josh Allen have welcomed their first child. The couple is blessed with a daughter.

Dhurandhar 2's 15-day run: Rs 1,492 crore box office; behind Pushpa 2, Baahubali 2 Dhurandhar: The Revenge has finished its two-week run at the box office, creating new records and breaking the existing ones - both globally and domestically. Mummy promiseRelease Date:Fri Apr 03, 2026 Maa Ka Sum series review: Maa Ka Sum explores the complexities of modern love using mathematics as a narrative tool but struggles to balance emotional depth with academic themes. Mona Singh delivers a strong performance, yet the series fails to fully engage viewers with its overcomplicated storytelling.

I was surprisingly calm, my family freaked out: Laetitia Hollard on landing The Pitt Actor Laetitia Hollard shared her emotional journey and the authenticity involved in portraying nurse Emma Nolan in the medical drama, The Pitt.

Tanuja crowns Aditya Dhar the real hero of Dhurandhar 2 in her review of film. Watch Kajol's mother and veteran actor Tanuja recently watched Dhurandhar: The Revenge and seemed deeply impressed with director Aditya Dhar's work. Her daughter Tanishaa Mukerji shared a video of her review.

general news.headline: Not Iran, Trump's USA is seeing a regime changeThe US launched the war against Iran, promising a regime change in Tehran. Instead, a regime change might be unfolding in Washington DC. Trump is firing top officers in what looks like an escalating purge. Experts say more heads might roll soon.

This comes a day after the party dropped Chadha as Rajya Sabha deputy leader.

Are people getting LPG cylinders easily? To find out, we spoke to people from across 12 states. Here's what they told us.

Live: BJP's list for Tamil Nadu out, ex-party chief Annamalai not includedAssembly Election News 2026 Live Updates: Election campaigning across four states and one Union Territory is in full swing. The counting of votes in Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, West Bengal and Puducherry will be held on May 4 (Monday). Elections have taken a curious turn in West Bengal, with the TMC alleging that voters from Bihar have been added to the state's electoral roll. In Kerala, the Congress has accused the ruling Left Front government of being the BJP's B-team. Meanwhile, the entry of Vijay-led TVK could change the electoral dynamics in Tamil Nadu, where the DMK and the AIADMK have taken turns to rule the state. In Assam, the Congress is looking to dislodge the BJP after being out of power for 10 years. On April 3, Home Minister Amit Shah will hold rallies in Assam while PM Narendra Modi will undertake a roadshow in Puducherry. Follow indiatoday.in for all the latest updates.

Bengal hostage row: AIMIM leader arrested, named 'mastermind' of protestsAIMIM leader Mofakkerul Islam was arrested at Bagdogra Airport for masterminding the Malda protest that led to the hostage-taking of seven judicial officers.

Iran claims to have hit second F-35 jet since conflict with US & Israel beganWhile there was no independent confirmation of the claim, the Iranian military officials said that the pilot may have been killed after the jet exploded on impact in Central Iran.

Pete Hegseth asks US Army chief Randy George to step down, retire immediatelyWhile the Pentagon has not given a reason for the firing, sources told CBS News that the move was driven by the Defence Secretary's desire for "someone who will implement President Donald Trump's vision for the Army."

Live: Debris from intercepted threat hit Habshan gas facilities, says Abu DhabiUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Iran-US-Israel conflict is intensifying, with tensions spreading across the region and new flashpoints emerging. Iran says the war will continue until its enemies face "humiliation" and "surrender," warning against any US ground invasion, while also claiming unverified strikes on the USS Abraham Lincoln and US drone units at Ali Al Salem Air Base. US President Donald Trump said Iran's largest bridge had been hit and urged Tehran to reach a deal "before it is too late," as US and Israeli attacks intensified, targeting sites in Tehran and key infrastructure. Despite Trump's claim that Iran sought a ceasefire, Tehran has denied it, with the IRGC adding that the Strait of Hormuz will remain closed to its enemies. Iran says more than 2,000 people have been killed and over 26,000 wounded since February 28. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest and live updates.

Rishi Sunak congratulates Indian-origin chess player, 11, on England's top rankIn a post on X, Rishi Sunak shared a picture with Bodhana Sivanandan, saying that her achievement did not come as a surprise to him.

India to get 3rd nuclear submarine? Rajnath Singh teases INS AridhamanDefence Minister Rajnath Singh hinted at the launch of INS Aridaman, India's third indigenously built nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine, in a social media post.

Silenced, not defeated: Raghav Chadha's video message blows the lid off AAP riftRaghav Chadha's outburst came a day after Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) replaced him as its deputy leader of the party in Rajya Sabha with Punjab MP Ashok Mittal amid buzz over his differences with the top leadership.

NCERT gets university status: Is this expansion or deeper control over education?NCERT is now a deemed university with powers to offer degrees and courses. While this could boost research and teacher training, the timing and recent controversies raise key questions about control, independence, and how education in India may be shaped going forward.

Does a luxury handbag made from dinosaur collagen interest you? It's up for auctionWho knew that a fragment of the prehistoric past could be reimagined as a future luxury product! Although it's not without raising a few scientific eyebrows.

Schools to hotels: How civilian sites have come under attack during Middle East warSince February 28, US-Israeli strikes in Iran and Tehran's retaliation across the Gulf nations have hit several civilian sites, including schools, hospitals, airports, and residential areas, with thousands reported dead and injured.

Indian astronomers discover forbidden alien planet that shouldn't existAn international research team, including astrophysicist Anjali Piette from the University of Birmingham, analysed data collected by the powerful James Webb Space Telescope.

He debated issues in the Rajya Sabha that people generally discuss on Reddit. The AAP MP has a huge social media following.

The golf legend has since stepped away from the sport to focus on his health.

Abhishek was sanctioned for breaching IPL's Code of Conduct during the game.

SRH bowling not weak as you think: Nitish Reddy shuts critics after KKR win IPL 2026: Nitish Kumar Reddy shut down criticism of SRH's bowling unit after a dominant win over KKR. The all-rounder insisted their attack is as strong as their much-hyped batting lineup.

IPL 2026 Play of the Day: How Angkrish's calls hurt KKR IPL 2026, KKR vs SRH: Kolkata Knight Riders' chase unraveled after a pair of costly misjudgments, as lapses in running between the wickets brought about their undoing with Cameron Green and Angkrish Raghuvanshi falling to avoidable run-outs that stalled the innings and handed the game firmly to Sunrisers Hyderabad.

Nitish's redemption night at Eden: From injury blues to SRH hero in Kolkata Nitish Kumar Reddy overcame self-doubt and injury woes to emerge as SRH'S hero at the Eden Gardens as they beat KKR. Nitish starred with both bat and ball to change the course of the game and get the win.

Expect Green to bowl very soon: Ajinkya Rahane shares exciting news for KKRAjinkya Rahane has shared a positive update about Cameron Green saying that the Australian all-rounder is set to bowl very soon in IPL 2026.

IPL chairman Arun Dhumal on Mustafizur Rahman's exit from KKR: 'Unfortunate'IPL chairman Arun Dhumal has finally spoken on Mustafizur Rahman's KKR omission, calling it "unfortunate" after the Bangladesh pacer was released following a BCCI directive amid India-Bangladesh tensions.

I would like to apologise to MS Dhoni, Kapil Dev: Yuvraj on father's remarksFormer India all-rounder Yuvraj Singh has apologised to MS Dhoni and Kapil Dev for the repeated remarks made by his father, Yograj Singh, saying in a podcast teaser that he does not support such statements.

Is USA safe to host FIFA World Cup after Iraq fans attacked at Dallas airport?There have been growing calls for FIFA to strip the United States of hosting rights for the 2026 World Cup after a video showing Iraqi fans being attacked at a Dallas airport went viral on social media.

Australia set for first Zimbabwe visit in 8 years to prepare for ODI World CupAustralian cricket team will return to Zimbabwe for the first time in 8 years to play a 3-match ODI series. This series will act as key preparation for Australia's ODI World Cup defence.

After Ahmedabad, India to get second 1 lakh capacity cricket stadiumMaharashtra's proposed 1 lakh-capacity stadium in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region aims to transform cricket infrastructure, boost tourism, and position the state as a global sporting hub. The initiative will be undertaken in collaboration with CIDCO and the Mumbai Cricket Association

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
